text.title: Axum router composition and route nesting
text.summary: Router composition pattern: per-domain route functions returning Router<AppState>, nested under /api/v1, with TraceLayer and global state attached at the top level.

section.title: Router composition

rule.statement: Define per-domain route functions (e.g. users::routes()) that return Router<AppState> without state. Compose them in a top-level api_routes() function. Attach state and layers only once at the top level in main.rs.
rule.rationale: Attaching state inside domain route functions makes them order-sensitive and hard to test independently. The top-level composition point is the single place where AppState is bound.

section.reference: Complete main.rs with router composition, TraceLayer, and state.

rule.statement: Promote to a Cargo workspace when: (a) cargo check takes >30s, or (b) a second binary (worker, admin CLI) is needed, or (c) a shared-types crate is required. Single-crate is correct until then.
rule.rationale: Premature workspace adds build complexity. The concrete signals above are measurable; "might need it" is not.
